documents.title: Insect-Pathogenic Fungus, Spores, Composition and Use of Same
documents.notes: Original filing carries [0001]-[00187] style paragraph markers. Use these directly as citation IDs during chunking.

documents.title: Pest Control Kit and Method
documents.notes: No bracketed paragraph numbers in this filing's extracted text. Citation IDs must be derived from SECTION/EXAMPLE headings instead (e.g. 'SECTION: EXAMPLE 4 - Control of vine weevil...'). Deliberately included to test the parser/chunker against a second real-world numbering convention, not just the clean bracketed case.

ingestion_notes: Both documents are granted/published patents, public record via Google Patents. No confidential Bionema material is used in this proof of concept.

F01 (Docling parse) must preserve paragraph markers for doc 1 and section/example headings for doc 2 as distinct citation-anchor conventions -- do not force both into one schema silently; store a 'locator_type' field per chunk.

F08 cross-document eval questions should include at least a few that can only be answered by comparing findings across both documents (e.g. formulation/delivery approach differences) to prove retrieval isn't just doc-1-only.
